title: Navasan.tech Latest FX/Gold-Coin Rates Snapshot
frequency: point-in-time (free tier has no push/streaming; re-run manually for future snapshots)
units: MIXED -- see notes. FX fields (e.g. harat_naghdi_sell/buy, usd_usdt) and per-gram gold fields (e.g. 18ayar) are in plain TOMAN (multiply by 10 for rial). Gold-COIN fields (bahar, sekkeh, nim, rob) are in THOUSANDS of toman (multiply by 10,000 for rial) -- confirmed empirically: raw 'bahar'=175000 and 'sekkeh'=179500 only match this project's own TGJU-sourced Bahar Azadi/Emami coin closes (~1.72-1.76 billion rial as of 2026-07-11) when multiplied by 10,000, not by 10. Cross-validated against brsapi's IR_COIN_BAHAR=175,020,000 toman and IR_COIN_EMAMI=179,505,000 toman from the same afternoon (2026-07-14 14:39 Tehran time), which agree with the x10,000-scaled navasan values to within a few hundred toman.
license_terms: Commercial API, free tier ~120 requests/month; data used here for corroboration/citation only, not redistribution of the raw feed.
notes: Key read from secrets/iran_api_keys.env (NAVASAN_KEY), never written to any repo file. Field harat_naghdi_sell=183240 toman (=1,832,400 rial) as of 1405-04-23 14:37 Tehran time corroborates this project's existing TGJU-sourced parallel-market USD series (fx__official_vs_parallel_gap_irn), whose last point was 1,782,000 rial on 2026-07-11 -- a plausible ~2.8% move over 3 days. bahar (Bahar Azadi coin, x10,000-scaled) = 1,750,000,000 rial vs chart's 2026-07-11 close of 1,721,800,000 rial (+1.6%, plausible). sekkeh (Emami coin, x10,000-scaled) = 1,795,000,000 rial vs chart's 2026-07-11 close of 1,760,100,000 rial (+2.0%, plausible).
